examples: One thing we know for sure — it is not the Higgs boson.
The Higgs singlet is related to another particle which is also yet to be found, the Higgs boson.
However, we do not know the mass of the Higgs boson itself, which makes it more difficult to identify.
Gluons colliding to produce a Higgs boson which then decays to a pair of photons.
The coupling of the Higgs boson to both W and Z bosons were observed.
